title: lepturus copeanus
intro: Lepturus copeanus, also known as Cope's grass, is a species of grass native to the Philippines and is classified under the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Lepturus copeanus include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Lepturus copeanus is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used in the form of a poultice or salve to promote healing and relieve pain.
